citeinfo.title: EPA Region Boundaries Exclusive Economic Zone, US, 2012, US EPA, SEGS

pubinfo.publish: U.S. EPA Office of Environmental Information (OEI) - Office of Information Analysis and Access (OIAA)

descript.abstract: This SEGS dataset delineates EPA Region boundaries extended to the 200nm exclusive economic zone and was created by U.S. EPA using 2011 TIGER/Line state boundaries from the U.S. Census Bureau, and U.S. Maritime Boundaries and EPA Regions made available by NOAA Office of Coast Survey. This dataset differs from the EPA Region layer offered by NOAA in that it uses 2012 TIGER/Line as the land boundaries.
descript.purpose: The core mission of Shared Enterprise Geodata and Services (SEGS) is to provide a single point of ownership for geospatial datasets that are national in extent and of general use to all EPA users and to make those datasets available through channels that best meet user needs.

place.placekt: ANSI INCITS 38:2009 (Formerly FIPS 5-2), ANSI INCITS 31:2009 (Formerly FIPS 6-4),ANSI INCITS 454:2009 (Formerly FIPS 8-6), ANSI INCITS 455:2009(Formerly FIPS 9-1), ANSI INCITS 446:2008 (Geographic Names Information System (GNIS))

idinfo.useconst: The TIGER/Line Shapefile products are not copyrighted however TIGER/Line and Census TIGER are registered trademarks of the U.S. Census Bureau. These products are free to use in a product or publication, however acknowledgement must be given to the U.S. Census Bureau as the source. The boundary information in the TIGER/Line Shapefiles are for statistical data collection and tabulation purposes only; their depiction and designation for statistical purposes does not constitute a determination of jurisdictional authority or rights of ownership or entitlement and they are not legal land descriptions.Coordinates in the TIGER/Line shapefiles have six implied decimal places, but the positional accuracy of these coordinates is not as great as the six decimal places suggest.
idinfo.Title_13_Restrictions: Yes, the data are free from Title 13 restrictions

cntorgp.cntorg: U.S. Department of Commerce, U.S. Census Bureau, Geography Division, Geographic Products Branch

attracc.attraccr: Accurate against Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS), FIPS Publication 6-4, and FIPS-55 at the 100% level for the codes and base names. The remaining attribute information has been examined but has not been fully tested for accuracy.

dataqual.logic: Tests for integrity have not been performed.
dataqual.complete: Features represented have not been tested for completeness

srcinfo.srccontr: The selected geographic and cartographic information (line segments) are derived from the U.S. Census Bureau's Master Address File Topologically Integrated Geographic Encoding and Referencing (MAF/TIGER) database.

citeinfo.origin: U.S. Department of Commerce, U.S. Census Bureau, Geography Division
citeinfo.title: Census MAF/TIGER database

procstep.procdesc: TIGER/Line Shapefiles are extracted from the Census MAF/TIGER database by nation, state, county, and entity. Census MAF/TIGER data for all of the aforementioned geographic entities are then distributed among the shapefiles each containing attributes for line, polygon, or landmark geographic data.
EPAREGION field was added and each state was assigned the value of its EPA region.
Layer was dissolved using EPAREGION as the dissolve field. All original attributes from TIGER were removed at this step.
USMaritimeLimitsNBoundariesSHP.zip was downloaded from NOAA (http://www.nauticalcharts.noaa.gov/csdl/mbound.htm) and EEZ line was extracted from the shapefile.
Gaps between EPA Regions and EEZ were removed by editing in ArcMap, extending the EEZ into EPA Regions.
EPA Regions and EEZ line were merged using Feature to Polygon tool in ArcMap 10.1 with an XY tolerance of 2 meters. This process added an EEZ buffer to the existing EPA Regions layer.
NOAA-created EPA Regions layer (Environmental Protection Agency Regions.shp) was downloaded from ftp://csc.noaa.gov/pub/Legis-Atlas/Federal_LegAtlas_Shapefiles.zip

NOAA-created EPA regions and EPA-created regions with EEZ polygons were overlain and the NOAA maritime regional boundaries were used as a template to cut the EEZ buffer into EPA Regions. The process used the Cut Polygon tool in an edit session.
Maritime polygons were attributed with their respective EPA region number.
Maritime polygons were dissolved into land-based EPA regions.

horizpa.horizpar: Data were collected using methods that have unknown accuracy (EPA National Geospatial Data Policy [NGDP] Accuracy Tier 10). For more information, please see EPA's NGDP at http://epa.gov/geospatial/policies.html)

spdoinfo.indspref: Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS), ANSI, and feature names.

geodetic.horizdn: North American Datum of 1983 in the 48 contiguous states, the District of Columbia, Alaska, Hawaii, Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands of the United States, and the Pacific Island Areas.

enttyp.enttypds: U.S. Census Bureau, NOAA and U.S. EPA

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.
Addresses and phone numbers for EPA regional facilities

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Area of feature in internal units squared.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

distinfo.distliab: No warranty, expressed or implied is made with regard to the accuracy of these data, and no liability is assumed by the U.S. Government in general or the U.S. Census Bureau in specific as to the spatial or attribute accuracy of the data. The act of distribution shall not constitute any such warranty and no responsibility is assumed by the U.S. government in the use of these files. The boundary information in the TIGER/Line Shapefiles is for statistical data collection and tabulation purposes only; their depiction and designation for statistical purposes do not constitute a determination of jurisdictional authority or rights of ownership or entitlement and they are not legal land descriptions.

stdorder.fees: The online copy of the TIGER/Line files may be accessed without charge.
stdorder.ordering: To obtain more information about ordering TIGER/Line shapefiles visit http://www.census.gov/geo/www/tiger

distinfo.techpreq: The TIGER/Line shapefiles contain geographic data only and do not include display mapping software or statistical data. For information on how to use the TIGER/Line shapefile data with specific software package users shall contact the company that produced the software.

cntorgp.cntorg: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Environmental Information

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata

cntorgp.cntorg: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, Office of Environmental Information

dataIdInfo.envirDesc: Microsoft Windows Vista Version 6.0 (Build 6002) Service Pack 2; ESRI ArcGIS 10.0.4.4000
